How BinDash Works

Every collection is two runner visits -- we handle both ends

1
Bin Out
Evening before collection day . runner moves bins to curb
->
🚛
Waste Management Collects
Your waste management service empties bins at curb
->
2
Bin In
Same day, after truck passes . runner returns bins to property
->
✓
All Done
You get a notification -- bins are safely back
🗺️
Smart Route Batching
Groups nearby properties into optimised routes. Runners cover more stops, faster.
🌙
Two Separate Batches
Bin-Out runs the evening before. Bin-In runs after the truck passes. Each is its own route -- different runners can cover each shift.
💵
Pay splits evenly across both visits. A runner doing both earns the full rate. Doing one visit earns half.
Flexible Runner Pay
🚨
SOS Emergency
Need bins out or in urgently? Emergency dispatch goes to the nearest available runner in minutes.
🏠
Homeowner Profiles
Gate codes, pets, bin location -- runners see everything they need before arriving.
📦
One-Time Callout
Need just one collection? Book a single Bin Out, Bin In, or both from $12.99. Recycling or general waste -- no subscription needed.

How it works

Simple shifts, consistent pay, total flexibility

🌙
Evening -- Bin Out
The night before collection day, move bins from properties to the curb. Routes are usually 6-10 properties, all within walking or cycling distance of each other. Takes about 45-60 minutes.
☀️
Morning -- Bin In
After the waste truck has passed, return bins to properties. You'll get a push notification when your zone unlocks. Same stops as the night before -- you already know the route.
📬
Offers come to you
BinDash sends batch offers 24 hours in advance. You have first refusal on your zone. Accept the whole batch at once -- both visits, one tap. No bidding, no guessing.
💵
Weekly payout
Earnings are calculated automatically and paid weekly to your bank account. You're an independent 1099 contractor -- set your own hours, no minimum commitment.

Grow your route, earn more

Your rate increases as your route grows. Recruit your neighbours and unlock higher pay permanently.

Starter
$3.25
per stop . per visit
1-9 stops
Signup bonus: $10 per signup
Builder
$3.50
per stop . per visit
10-19 stops
Signup bonus: $12 per signup
MOST REACH
Established
$3.75
per stop . per visit
20-29 stops
Signup bonus: $15 per signup
Elite
$4.00
per stop . per visit
30+ stops
Signup bonus: $15 + zone royalty
🔗
Your referral link
Every runner gets a personal referral link tied to their zone. Any homeowner who signs up through your link joins your default route permanently -- recurring weekly income, not a one-time gig.
📈
Zone steward bonus
Once your zone is dense enough to support a second runner, you earn a 4% royalty on their stop earnings. You built that density -- you share in it.
📣
BinDash backs you
We run local ads, postcard drops, and Nextdoor campaigns targeted at zones close to unlock thresholds. Signups from those campaigns in your zone count toward your route and bonus totals.

How many bins?
Every plan includes:
🌙 Bin Out -- runner moves your bins to the curb the evening before collection

Your home location sets a boundary (res-6 hex ~36 km²). Tap res-7 hexes inside it to select the areas you're willing to cover. Hexes outside your boundary are locked — this prevents you being assigned impossibly far routes.

Availability & Visit Preference 📅
BinDash has two types of runner visits. You can opt into one or both.
🌙 BIN OUT
Evening shift
The night before collection day. Move bins from property to curb.
$3.25/stop
☀️ BIN IN
Daytime shift
After the truck has passed. Return bins from curb to property.
$3.25/stop
💡 Do both visits at the same property and earn $6.50/stop. The algorithm will try to match you to the same properties for both your Out and In runs.
Which visits will you do?
🌙☀️
Both visits
$6.50/stop max

Bin Out only

☀️
Bin In only

Independent Contractor 📄
BinDash runners are 1099 independent contractors.
⚠️ Important: You are an independent contractor, not an employee. You are responsible for your own taxes. BinDash will issue a 1099-NEC if you earn over $600 in a calendar year.
🔒
Tax info collected securely after approval
We don't collect your SSN or bank details here. Once your application is approved, you'll set up payouts through Stripe — the same secure payment platform used by millions of businesses. Stripe handles all tax identity verification (W-9) and bank setup with full encryption.
✓ Bank-level encryption
✓ 1099-NEC issued automatically
✓ Direct bank deposit
Agreements
BinDash Contractor Agreement (Summary)

You are an independent contractor. You set your own schedule and may work elsewhere. You are responsible for equipment, insurance, and your own tax obligations. BinDash issues a 1099-NEC for earnings over $600/year. You agree to professional conduct with all homeowners and consent to BinDash's platform policies.
I understand I am a 1099 independent contractor and responsible for my own taxes
I agree to the BinDash Code of Conduct and platform policies
I consent to a background check as part of the approval process
Review & Submit 🎯
Your application goes to Pending Review -- approved within 24-48 hours.
📋 Once approved you'll be added to the dispatch pool for your selected visit types.

Notification Preferences
Runner on the way

Collection complete

Collection day check-in OPTIONAL
An email on your collection day when your bins have been placed at the curb, and again when they've been returned. Helps us keep your route on schedule.
📋 This is completely optional and has no effect on your service. Responses are used anonymously with other homeowners in your zone to learn typical collection times. Not required, never used to track you individually.

Application Submitted!
Thanks for applying to become a BinDash runner. We'll review your application and get back to you within 24-48 hours.
What happens next
1 Our team reviews your details, background check consent, and service area
2 You're assigned to a service zone and added to the dispatch pool

🚀
You're on the map!
Your application is in, and we've created a waitlist zone for your area. You're the first runner to stake a claim here.
⏳ How your zone activates
1 Your application gets approved by admin — no work starts yet
2 Homeowners in your ZIP sign up to BinDash and join the waitlist — 3 homeowners needed
3 Zone goes live — you get notified and your first routes begin
💡 Share BinDash with your neighbours — every homeowner who signs up in your ZIP brings your zone closer to launch. Use your referral link once you're approved.
📧 We'll email you at the address you provided. Check your spam folder if you don't hear from us within 48 hours.

📡 Scout Area
Your zones & coverage — tap hexes to manage
Your active zone is shown in cyan. Tap any green res-8 hex inside it to toggle coverage on/off. Your blue res-6 boundary is your default zone. Changes save automatically.
Tap any greyed res-6 zone outside your area to request it. You will be added as a secondary runner once admin approves. Zones with 🟡 amber hexes have homeowner demand — good targets.

ZIP codes where homeowners signed up but no zone exists yet. Review demand and decide whether to open a zone.
No waitlisted zones yet.
🗺️ Zone Requests
Runners request coverage for new ZIPs. Review, create zones, and assign runners here.
How it works: Runners on the road flag neighborhoods where they see demand — multiple bins out, neighbors asking about service, clusters of interest. Each flag creates or increments a micro zone signal. When enough flags accumulate for a ZIP, it surfaces here for you to review and potentially activate a new zone.
Loading scout reports...
Homeowners who signed up in areas BinDash doesn't yet service. When a runner activates in their ZIP, notify them to complete enrollment.
No waitlist signups yet.
People who requested a runner invite code. Review their details, approve to generate a unique code -- it will display here and can be shared with them directly.
No runner requests yet.
Review new homeowner applications. Verify their address, assign them to a zone, confirm service details, then approve to activate their account.
No pending homeowner applications.
Review runner applications. Check 1099 agreements, background check consent, assign a service zone, then approve to add them to the dispatch pool.
